dependencies.description: NAS must reach RADIUS server on UDP 1812 (auth), 1813 (accounting). Server must reach NAS on UDP 1700 or 3799 for CoA/Disconnect. Stateless UDP — no TCP handshake.
dependencies.name: UDP reachability to RADIUS server

dependencies.description: IOS-XE: 'aaa new-model' required. NX-OS: 'feature tacacs+' / 'feature radius' not strictly needed but RADIUS is default for dot1x. IOS-XR: AAA enabled by default.
dependencies.name: AAA model enabled

dependencies.description: NAS and RADIUS server must have identical shared secrets. Used to encrypt User-Password attribute and authenticate responses (Response Authenticator).
dependencies.name: Shared secret configured on both sides

dependencies.description: NAS must have IP route to RADIUS server. VRF-aware config needed if RADIUS server is in a management VRF.
dependencies.name: IP routing / reachability

dependencies.description: RADIUS server (ISE) must have the NAS registered as a network device with matching IP and shared secret.
dependencies.name: Network device entry on RADIUS server

failure_modes.description: RADIUS shared key differs between NAS and server — server silently drops requests or NAS rejects responses with bad authenticator

resolutions.action: Compare shared secret on both NAS and RADIUS server character-by-character
Compare shared secret on both NAS and RADIUS server character-by-character
Compare shared secret on both NAS and RADIUS server character-by-character
On NX-OS: 'show run | inc radius' (secret shown as type-7, decrypt to verify)
On NX-OS: 'show run | inc radius' (secret shown as type-7, decrypt to verify)
On NX-OS: 'show run | inc radius' (secret shown as type-7, decrypt to verify)
On IOS-XE: 'show run | inc radius' or 'more system:running-config | inc key'
On IOS-XE: 'show run | inc radius' or 'more system:running-config | inc key'
On IOS-XE: 'show run | inc radius' or 'more system:running-config | inc key'
Re-enter secret on both sides — avoid copy-paste, type manually
Re-enter secret on both sides — avoid copy-paste, type manually
Re-enter secret on both sides — avoid copy-paste, type manually
Test with 'test aaa group <group> <user> <pass> new-code' (IOS-XE)
Test with 'test aaa group <group> <user> <pass> new-code' (IOS-XE)
Test with 'test aaa group <group> <user> <pass> new-code' (IOS-XE)
Check ISE: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices > specific device
Check ISE: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices > specific device
Check ISE: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices > specific device

root_causes.cause: Shared secret misconfigured on NAS or RADIUS server
Copy-paste error with trailing whitespace/newline in secret
Secret contains special characters that shell interprets differently
ISE network device entry has different secret than switch config
Key rollover in progress — old and new keys mixed

symptoms: Authentication always times out (server drops malformed request)
'show aaa servers' shows timeouts incrementing but no rejects
debug radius shows 'invalid message authenticator' or 'bad authenticator in response'
RADIUS server logs show 'shared secret mismatch' or 'bad authenticator'

Verify basic connectivity: 'ping <radius-ip> source <source-intf>'

Check RADIUS server service status

Verify firewall rules for UDP 1812, 1813, 1700/3799 (CoA)
Verify firewall rules for UDP 1812, 1813, 1700/3799 (CoA)
Check source-interface config: 'ip radius source-interface' (IOS-XE)
Check source-interface config: 'ip radius source-interface' (IOS-XE)
Check source-interface config: 'ip radius source-interface' (IOS-XE)
For VRF-aware: ensure RADIUS server in correct VRF context
For VRF-aware: ensure RADIUS server in correct VRF context
For VRF-aware: ensure RADIUS server in correct VRF context

Configure deadtime + automate-tester for faster failover

root_causes.cause: RADIUS server down or crashed
Network path failure (routing, interface down)
Firewall blocking UDP 1812/1813 (auth/acct) or UDP 1700/3799 (CoA)
RADIUS server listening on wrong interface/IP
Source interface on NAS not configured or wrong VRF
NAS source IP not matching the network device entry on ISE

symptoms: All authentications timing out
'show aaa servers' shows all servers dead or high timeout counts
New users/devices cannot authenticate
Existing sessions may continue until re-auth (if authorized)

failure_modes.description: Without deadtime, NAS keeps trying dead servers on every authentication attempt, causing long delays for all users

resolutions.action: Configure deadtime: 'radius-server deadtime 5' (5-15 min recommended)
Configure deadtime: 'radius-server deadtime 5' (5-15 min recommended)
Configure deadtime: 'radius-server deadtime 5' (5-15 min recommended)
IOS-XE: configure dead-criteria: 'radius-server dead-criteria time 10 tries 3'
IOS-XE: configure dead-criteria: 'radius-server dead-criteria time 10 tries 3'
IOS-XE: configure dead-criteria: 'radius-server dead-criteria time 10 tries 3'
IOS-XE: configure automate-tester: 'radius-server host <ip> automate-tester username <user> probe-on'
IOS-XE: configure automate-tester: 'radius-server host <ip> automate-tester username <user> probe-on'
IOS-XE: configure automate-tester: 'radius-server host <ip> automate-tester username <user> probe-on'
Consider reducing retransmit timeout and count for faster failover
Consider reducing retransmit timeout and count for faster failover
Consider reducing retransmit timeout and count for faster failover

root_causes.cause: radius-server deadtime not configured (default 0 = disabled)
Dead-criteria not configured — server never marked dead
No automate-tester — NAS cannot detect server recovery proactively

symptoms: Authentication takes 15-45+ seconds when primary server is down
Users experience long login delays
Timeouts on primary before falling through to secondary
'show aaa servers' shows timeouts on primary but eventually succeeds on secondary

resolutions.action: Verify CoA port: 'show run | inc coa' — ensure 'aaa server radius dynamic-author'
Verify CoA port: 'show run | inc coa' — ensure 'aaa server radius dynamic-author'
Verify CoA port: 'show run | inc coa' — ensure 'aaa server radius dynamic-author'

IOS-XE: configure 'aaa server radius dynamic-author' with correct client IP and key

NX-OS: configure 'radius-server host <ip> authentication accounting' with CoA support
NX-OS: configure 'radius-server host <ip> authentication accounting' with CoA support
Check firewall permits inbound UDP 1700 (or 3799) to NAS management IP
Check firewall permits inbound UDP 1700 (or 3799) to NAS management IP
Check firewall permits inbound UDP 1700 (or 3799) to NAS management IP
Verify ISE CoA settings: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices
Verify ISE CoA settings: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices
Verify ISE CoA settings: Administration > Network Resources > Network Devices

Match NAS-IP-Address: ensure source-interface on NAS aligns with ISE device entry
Match NAS-IP-Address: ensure source-interface on NAS aligns with ISE device entry

root_causes.cause: CoA port mismatch — NAS expects 1700 (legacy) or 3799 (RFC 5176)
Firewall blocking inbound UDP 1700/3799 to NAS
Source IP in CoA doesn't match configured RADIUS server on NAS
CoA shared secret different from auth secret (some platforms)
NAS source-interface not matching ISE's expected NAS-IP-Address
Session-ID in CoA doesn't match active session on NAS

symptoms: ISE shows CoA timeout or CoA NAK in live logs
Policy changes on ISE don't take effect on NAS
VLAN/ACL changes require manual session clear on NAS
Posture flow stalls — endpoint stays in REDIRECT state

root_causes.cause: RADIUS attribute name/format incorrect for this NAS platform
VLAN returned by RADIUS doesn't exist on NAS
dACL name doesn't match any ACL on NAS (or dACL download failed)
NAS doesn't support the returned attribute (e.g., SGT on older code)
cisco-av-pair syntax incorrect (common: missing colon or space)
Attribute returned in wrong RADIUS message (must be in Access-Accept)

symptoms: User authenticated but in wrong VLAN
Downloadable ACL not applied
SGT not assigned despite ISE policy returning it
'show authentication sessions interface <intf> details' shows missing attributes

resolutions.action: For EAP: use failover (ordered) not load-balance — EAP requires session stickiness
For EAP: use failover (ordered) not load-balance — EAP requires session stickiness
For EAP: use failover (ordered) not load-balance — EAP requires session stickiness
If load-balancing needed: use external LB (F5/Radware) with RADIUS session stickiness
If load-balancing needed: use external LB (F5/Radware) with RADIUS session stickiness
If load-balancing needed: use external LB (F5/Radware) with RADIUS session stickiness
IOS-XE: 'load-balance method least-outstanding batch-size 5' in server group
IOS-XE: 'load-balance method least-outstanding batch-size 5' in server group
IOS-XE: 'load-balance method least-outstanding batch-size 5' in server group
Alternatively: use RADIUS proxy (e.g., ISE RADIUS proxy) for session-aware LB
Alternatively: use RADIUS proxy (e.g., ISE RADIUS proxy) for session-aware LB
Alternatively: use RADIUS proxy (e.g., ISE RADIUS proxy) for session-aware LB
Check ISE: ensure all PSN nodes have same policy configuration
Check ISE: ensure all PSN nodes have same policy configuration
Check ISE: ensure all PSN nodes have same policy configuration

root_causes.cause: Server group configured with load-balance but RADIUS is stateful for EAP
EAP multi-round exchanges split across servers (no session stickiness)
Load-balancer in front of ISE without sticky sessions
Batch size or method-list configuration causing poor distribution

symptoms: EAP authentications randomly fail when multi-round exchange hits different servers
Some RADIUS servers overloaded while others idle
'show aaa servers' shows very uneven request distribution
ISE shows incomplete EAP sessions in failed attempts

ISE network device entry has wrong NAS-IP-Address
Multiple IPs on NAS — RADIUS picks unexpected source

root_causes.cause: Accounting not configured on NAS
Accounting server unreachable when session ended
NAS reloaded without sending accounting-stop for active sessions
aaa accounting method list missing 'stop-only' or 'start-stop'
Network event (interface flap) causes session teardown without accounting

messages.description: NAS sends authentication request to RADIUS server with user credentials and NAS attributes
messages.name: Access-Request
messages.when_sent: When a user/device needs authentication (login, 802.1X, VPN, etc.)

messages.description: RADIUS server grants access — may include authorization attributes (VLAN, ACL, SGT, etc.)
key_fields: cisco-av-pair VSA — SGT, dACL, URL-redirect, etc.
Class (25) — session identifier for accounting correlation
messages.name: Access-Accept
messages.when_sent: Authentication and authorization policy check succeeded

key_fields: EAP-Message (79) — EAP-Failure if EAP was used
messages.name: Access-Reject
messages.when_sent: Credentials invalid or authorization policy denied

messages.description: RADIUS server requests additional information — used for multi-round EAP exchanges (PEAP, EAP-TLS, EAP-FAST)
key_fields: State (24) — opaque state to correlate multi-round exchange
messages.name: Access-Challenge
messages.when_sent: During EAP negotiation when more rounds needed

messages.description: NAS sends accounting record (start, interim-update, or stop)
messages.name: Accounting-Request
messages.when_sent: Start: session begins. Interim: periodic update. Stop: session ends.

messages.description: RADIUS server pushes policy change to NAS — reauthenticate, change ACL/VLAN, or bounce port
messages.name: CoA-Request (RFC 5176)
messages.when_sent: Administrator changes policy on ISE/RADIUS, or posture status changes, or profiling reclassifies endpoint

messages.description: RADIUS server instructs NAS to terminate a specific session
key_fields: Acct-Session-Id (44) — identifies which session to terminate
messages.name: Disconnect-Request (RFC 5176)
messages.when_sent: Administrator forces session termination from RADIUS server

purpose: Client/server protocol for centralized authentication, authorization, and accounting — the transport layer for 802.1X, device admin, and VPN authentication

states.description: No pending RADIUS transaction — NAS ready to send new requests
states.name: IDLE

states.description: Access-Request or Accounting-Request sent to RADIUS server, waiting for response
states.name: REQUEST_SENT

states.description: No response received within timeout, retransmitting request to same or next server
states.name: RETRANSMITTING

states.description: Access-Accept received — user/device authenticated successfully. NAS applies returned attributes (VLAN, ACL, SGT, etc.)
states.name: ACCEPTED

states.description: Access-Reject received — credentials or policy check failed
states.name: REJECTED

states.description: Access-Challenge received — multi-round EAP exchange in progress (e.g., PEAP, EAP-TLS handshake)
states.name: CHALLENGED

states.description: RADIUS server marked dead after consecutive timeouts — NAS will skip this server until deadtime expires
states.name: SERVER_DEAD

states.description: Change of Authorization request received from RADIUS server (RFC 5176) — NAS processing policy change or disconnect
states.name: COA_RECEIVED

timers.description: Time to wait for RADIUS server response before retransmitting
timers.name: retransmit-timeout
timers.on_expiry: Retransmit the request or try next server

os_defaults.notes: Configured via 'radius-server timeout' or per-server. With automate-tester, dead-criteria uses this value.
Configured under 'radius-server host' or 'aaa group server radius'

timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster failover (2-3s recommended with multiple servers). Must account for EAP round-trips on the server side.

timers.description: Number of retransmissions before marking server dead
timers.name: retransmit-count
timers.on_expiry: Server marked dead after this many consecutive timeouts
timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster failover. With 3 servers at 5s timeout and 3 retries, worst-case auth delay is 45s. Recommend 1-2 retries with 2-3s timeout for responsive failover.

timers.description: Time a RADIUS server remains marked dead after consecutive timeouts — requests skip dead servers during this period
timers.name: deadtime
timers.on_expiry: Server returned to active pool and probed again

os_defaults.notes: Also supports 'radius-server dead-criteria' with time + tries thresholds. Use 'radius-server deadtime' globally or per-server.
Configured under 'radius-server deadtime' or per-server in the aaa group

timers.tuning_advice: ALWAYS configure deadtime (5-15 min recommended). Without deadtime, NAS keeps trying dead servers and every auth suffers the full timeout chain. Critical for failover scenarios.

timers.description: Timeout for accounting requests — separate from auth timeout on some platforms
timers.name: accounting-timeout
timers.tuning_advice: Accounting can tolerate longer timeouts since it's not in the user auth path. But lost accounting-stop records cause phantom sessions on ISE.

timers.description: IOS-XE: interval for sending test probes to RADIUS servers to detect recovery from dead state
timers.name: automate-tester-interval
timers.on_expiry: Sends a test auth request to probe dead servers

os_defaults.notes: Configured via 'automate-tester username <user> probe-on'. Requires a test user identity. Sends periodic test-auth to dead servers to detect recovery.

timers.tuning_advice: Use 'radius-server dead-criteria time <s> tries <n>' with 'automate-tester' to detect server recovery faster than waiting for deadtime to expire naturally.

transitions.trigger: Authentication or accounting event triggers RADIUS request
Access-Challenge received — EAP method exchange in progress

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check RADIUS server reachability, UDP 1812/1813 not blocked, shared secret correct (server silently drops on mismatch)

transitions.trigger: NAS forwards next EAP frame from supplicant as new Access-Request
Final Access-Accept received after EAP exchange completes
Retransmit sent (same server or next server in group)
All retransmits exhausted for this server — server marked dead, deadtime starts
Deadtime expires — server returned to active pool for probing
Session complete or accounting stop sent
Rejection processed, NAS returns to idle
Unsolicited CoA or Disconnect-Request received from RADIUS server (UDP 1700/3799)
CoA processed — CoA-ACK or CoA-NAK sent back to server

notes.note: IOS-XE RADIUS: most feature-rich implementation. Supports 'aaa new-model' (required), 'radius server <name>' (named server config), server-groups, per-server keys, dead-criteria, automate-tester, and 'test aaa group <group> <user> <pass> new-code' for live testing.

IOS-XE dead-server detection: 'radius-server dead-criteria time <s> tries <n>' + 'radius-server deadtime <min>'. Combined with 'automate-tester username <user> probe-on', this provides proactive dead-server detection and recovery. CRITICAL for production deployments.

IOS-XE CoA: 'aaa server radius dynamic-author' is required for ISE CoA/Disconnect. Configure client IP and key. Default CoA port is 1700 (Cisco legacy); RFC 5176 uses 3799. ISE defaults to 1700 for Cisco devices.

IOS-XE named RADIUS server syntax (modern): 'radius server <name>' / 'address ipv4 <ip> auth-port 1812 acct-port 1813' / 'key <secret>'. Old syntax 'radius-server host' still works but deprecated. Method lists reference server groups, not individual servers.

IOS-XR RADIUS: configured under 'radius-server host <ip>' or 'aaa group server radius <name>'. Source interface via 'source-interface <intf>' under group or global. VRF-aware via 'vrf <vrf-name>' in server group.

IOS-XR CoA: supported but less common — IOS-XR devices are typically routers not access switches. CoA configured under 'aaa server radius dynamic-author'. Used primarily for subscriber management (BNG) scenarios.
IOS-XR dead-server detection: 'radius-server deadtime <min>' supported. No automate-tester equivalent — relies on deadtime expiry for server re-probing.

RADIUS on NX-OS: 'radius-server host <ip> key <secret>'. Supports 'test aaa server radius <ip> <user> <pass>' for live testing. Server groups via 'aaa group server radius'. Source interface via 'ip radius source-interface'. NX-OS uses RADIUS primarily for dot1x — device admin typically uses TACACS+.
NX-OS VRF-aware RADIUS: use 'use-vrf <vrf-name>' under 'aaa group server radius' or globally. Common for mgmt VRF. Without this, RADIUS traffic uses default VRF and may fail.
NX-OS directed-request: 'radius-server directed-request' allows specifying RADIUS server in username (user@server). Useful for testing but security concern in production.
